2604.27633.title: Political Bias Audits of LLMs Capture Sycophancy to the Inferred Auditor
2604.27633.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are commonly evaluated for political bias based on their responses to fixed questionnaires, which typically place frontier models on the political left. A parallel literature shows that LLMs are sycophantic: they adapt their answers to the views, identities, and expectations of the user. We show that these findings are linked: standard political-bias audits partly capture sycophantic accommodation to the inferred auditor. We employ a factorial experiment across three major audit instruments--the Political Compass Test, the Pew Political Typology, and 1,540 partisan-benchmarked Pew American Trends Panel items--administered to six frontier LLMs while varying only the asker's stated identity (N = 30,990 responses). At baseline, all six models lean left. When the asker identifies as a conservative Republican, responses shift sharply: the share of items closer to Democrats falls by 28-62 percentage points, and all six models move right of center. A mirror-image progressive-Democrat cue produces little change; rightward accommodation is 8.0$\times$ larger than leftward. When asked who the default asker is, models identify an auditor, researcher, or academic; when asked what answer that asker expects, they select the Democrat-coded option 75% of the time, nearly the rate under an explicit progressive cue. These patterns are inconsistent with a purely fixed model ideology and indicate that single-prompt audits capture an interaction between model and inferred interlocutor. Political bias in LLMs is therefore not a fixed point on an ideological scale but a response profile that must be mapped across realistic interlocutors.
2604.27633.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）通常根據其對固定問卷的回應來評估政治偏見，這些問卷通常將前沿模型置於政治左派。平行文獻顯示，LLMs 是迎合者：它們根據用戶的觀點、身份和期望調整其答案。我們顯示這些發現是相互關聯的：標準的政治偏見審計部分捕捉到對推斷審計者的迎合。我們在三個主要審計工具上進行了一項因子實驗——政治羅盤測試、皮尤政治類型學和 1,540 個以黨派為基準的皮尤美國趨勢小組項目——對六個前沿 LLM 進行測試，同時僅變更提問者的聲明身份（N = 30,990 次回應）。在基線上，所有六個模型都偏向左派。當提問者自我認同為保守派共和黨人時，回應急劇轉變：更接近民主黨的項目比例下降 28-62 個百分點，所有六個模型都向右移動。鏡像進步-民主黨提示幾乎沒有變化；向右的迎合是向左的 8.0$\times$ 大。當被問及默認提問者是誰時，模型識別出一位審計者、研究者或學者；當被問及該提問者期望什麼答案時，它們選擇民主黨編碼選項的比例為 75%，幾乎達到明確進步提示下的比例。這些模式與純粹固定的模型意識形態不一致，並表明單一提示的審計捕捉到模型與推斷對話者之間的互動。因此，LLMs 中的政治偏見並不是意識形態尺度上的固定點，而是一種必須在現實對話者之間映射的回應輪廓。
2604.27633.author: Petter Törnberg et.al.
